lessonsLearned.title: Parallelism is 60% faster than sequential
lessonsLearned.description: 5 bots running parallel = ~8 min total. Sequential = ~20 min. Lesson: Always use parallelism when possible.
lessonsLearned.context: ENEM Game workflow with 5 HTTP requests
lessonsLearned.solution: Connect all 5 outputs from trigger node, then consolidate

lessonsLearned.title: Consolidate Results node is essential
lessonsLearned.description: Without consolidate, multiple outputs remain separate JSON objects. With consolidate, they merge into single array. Essential for processing parallel results.
lessonsLearned.solution: Add Consolidate Results → Spreadsheet Parser before final node

lessonsLearned.title: Rate limiting prevents API blocks
lessonsLearned.description: APIs block requests if too many in quick succession. Minimum 2 seconds between requests to same domain. Use delay nodes.
lessonsLearned.solution: Add Delay node between HTTP requests (2-5 seconds)

lessonsLearned.title: Phi3 is better than Qwen 32B for workflows
lessonsLearned.description: Qwen 2.5 32B requires 32GB RAM (crashes on 16GB). Phi3 requires 4GB. For N8n workflows, always use Phi3. Qwen 32B only for heavy compute.
lessonsLearned.solution: Model: phi3 (or llama3.1:8b for quality). Avoid qwen2.5:32b in N8n

lessonsLearned.title: Cron triggers are production-ready
lessonsLearned.description: Manual triggers need manual clicks. Cron triggers run 24/7 without intervention. For any recurring task, use cronTrigger. Much more reliable.
lessonsLearned.solution: Use cronTrigger with {hour: 0, minute: 0} for midnight execution

lessonsLearned.title: 🔴 CRÍTICO: N8N_USER_FOLDER deve ser definido sempre
lessonsLearned.description: N8n sem N8N_USER_FOLDER usa ~/.n8n como default. Se os workflows foram criados com uma variável diferente, o processo não enxerga os workflows existentes e a API retorna 0 workflows. Isso gera confusão de 'workflows sumiram'.
lessonsLearned.context: N8n API retornava 0 workflows. DB correto era ~/.n8n/.n8n/database.sqlite mas processo usava ~/.n8n/database.sqlite (vazio).
lessonsLearned.solution: SEMPRE iniciar N8n com: N8N_USER_FOLDER=/caminho/correto node /usr/local/bin/n8n start --insecure. Criar LaunchAgent com EnvironmentVariables para garantir permanência.
lessonsLearned.code:
# Comando correto:
export N8N_USER_FOLDER=/Volumes/TITA_039/MAC_MINI_03/.n8n
node /usr/local/bin/n8n start --insecure

# Para verificar qual DB está sendo usado:
find ~/.n8n -name 'database.sqlite' | while read f; do
  count=$(python3 -c "import sqlite3; c=sqlite3.connect('$f').cursor(); c.execute('SELECT COUNT(*) FROM workflow_entity'); print(c.fetchone()[0])" 2>/dev/null)
  echo "$count workflows: $f"
done

lessonsLearned.title: API Key N8n: usar /rest/login não funciona com password vazio
lessonsLearned.description: N8n --insecure mode ainda requer API key para endpoints /api/v1/. Usar X-N8N-API-KEY header. Chaves ficam em user_api_keys table no sqlite.
lessonsLearned.context: Tentei /rest/login sem senha, retornou erro. Solução: pegar API key direto do sqlite.
lessonsLearned.solution: python3 -c "import sqlite3; conn=sqlite3.connect('/path/.n8n/database.sqlite'); c=conn.cursor(); c.execute('SELECT label, apiKey FROM user_api_keys'); print(c.fetchall())"

lessonsLearned.title: Dashboard chat endpoint correto: /api/squad/:id/task
lessonsLearned.description: O endpoint de chat dos especialistas não é /api/chat nem /api/specialist/chat. É POST /api/squad/:specialistId/task com body {task, context}.
lessonsLearned.context: Testei /api/chat, /api/squad/chat, /api/specialist/chat — todos 404. Endpoint certo descoberto lendo index.ts do backend.
